A key feature of this app is its use of AI‑driven natural language generation to produce clear, human‑readable IOC summaries and campaign summaries, making threat intelligence more accessible to both analysts and decision‑makers.
8073.displayName: Threat Intel Enrichment AI App for Splunk
8073.name: ti_ai_enrich_for_splunk

8079.description: The Splunk Threat Intelligence App streamlines security operations by automating the enrichment and response of threat intelligence within Splunk. It extracts and de-duplicates key indicators of compromise (IOCs)—including IP addresses, domains, and file hashes—from raw security logs and submits them to the threatbook.io API for threat verdiction. Malicious IOCs are enriched with detailed intelligence and stored in a user-defined target index, while a dedicated dashboard provides clear visibility into threats across the environment. Analysts can correlate enriched intelligence with raw logs for deeper investigation and leverage the data to drive automated response actions, such as blocking malicious entities. Built-in de-duplication and API-aware processing ensure efficiency and optimized performance.
8079.name: threatbook-api

8090.description: Trigger NetBrain next-gen no-code automation built on Intents and ADT directly from Splunk alerts. Designed for single-domain deployments, it automates troubleshooting, mapping, and path analysis.
8090.name: netbrain_trigger

8092.description:
This is a Splunk Modular Alert for sending messages to Bale messenger using the Bale API.

The Python code in this App is Python 3 compatible.
This version of the App enforces Python 3 for execution of the alert action script when running on Splunk 8+ in order to satisfy Splunkbase AppInspect requirements.

Browse to the Bale Docs and read documents to find out how to build bot and get bot token and chat id.
Then used your bot token and chat id to config custom alert action.
Also you can use "Result tokens" like $result.fieldname$ for this alert action e.g. in message textbox

This App is built and supported by Meysam Talebi.
8092.name: bale_message

8095.description:
Threat Intel OSINT Feeds for Splunk is a Splunk app that automates the collection of open‑source threat intelligence (OSINT) feeds and ingests them into Splunk for security monitoring, enrichment, and correlation.
Use https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/8073 for Enrichment
8095.displayName: Threat Intel OSINT Feeds for Splunk
8095.name: ti_feeds_for_splunk

8097.description:
The InSitzes App for Splunk Cloud is a unified monitoring suite that gives administrators actionable insight into ingestion, system performance, workload management, search efficiency, storage, and compute consumption across their Splunk Cloud environment. Rather than hopping between the Cloud Monitoring Console, license pages, and ad-hoc searches, admins get one React-powered dashboard with 12 purpose-built tabs to answer the questions that matter most: Is my environment healthy? Is data flowing reliably? Are searches running efficiently? Where are my SVCs being consumed? How fast is storage growing, and do I have enough capacity to meet demand?

The Health tab runs 31 automated checks across 8 categories — System, Ingestion, Data Quality, Search, Compute, Storage, Workload, Forwarding, and Capacity — with weighted severity scoring and a rolled-up environment health score so you know at a glance where to focus. Individual tabs drill into SVC attribution by user, app, search head, provenance and search type; DDAS and DDAA storage utilization and forecasting with an optional Cost Mode; scheduled search skip reasons and wasteful-search detection; WLM filtered, reclassified, and aborted search rates; HEC throughput and SSL connectivity issues; dashboard refresh frequency and chain-search analysis; indexer cache churn and queue health; and license headroom trends. A guided Remediation tab translates every health finding into concrete next steps.

All charts render client-side as SVG so dashboards export cleanly to PDF for executive reporting. Ingestion data is read live from _internal license_usage events, eliminating scheduled-lookup maintenance. Pre-built alert saved searches (disabled by default) are included for ingestion anomalies, storage utilization, app updates, large lookup files, and redundant scheduled searches. The app helps teams proactively detect issues, optimize resource usage, and align capacity to real business demand.
8097.name: insitzes_app_for_splunk_cloud

8099.description:
This app uses the 1Password CLI tool to regularly pull lists of users and groups. This can be useful if you want to track seat usage, user or group growth, or calculate statistics on which active users have signed in recently.

Special thanks to Martin Müller for his help troubleshooting this add-on.
8099.name: TA-1password-cli-tool

8100.description: This technical add-on is designed to allow CrowdStrike customers to collect and index detections from the CrowdStrike Falcon Platform via the combined alerts v1 API endpoint.
8100.name: TA_crowdstrike_falcon_detections

8105.description:
The Elastic Defend Add-on for Splunk provides comprehensive integration between
Elastic EDR/Defend and Splunk, enabling security teams to centralize endpoint
detection and response (EDR) data within their Splunk environment.
8105.name: TA-Elastic_defend

8106.description: This app uses the Nexthink API to trigger Remote actions or Campaings. This would require that the Nexthink device UID and user SID to be available.
8106.displayName: Sentia Alert App for running Nexthink Remote Actions and Campaings
8106.name: splunk_nexthink_alert

8107.description:
Get Public IP Add-on by zuykn.io - Collects the external public IPv4 and/or IPv6 egress addresses in use by a Splunk Universal Forwarder/Enterprise for Windows, Linux, Unix, and macOS.

Need help? Email us: support@zuykn.io or visit us on the web: https://zuykn.io
8107.name: TA-get_public_ip

8109.description:
EC DIGIT CSIRC Add-on for Sysdiagnose Analysis Framework (SAF) is a Splunk Technical Addon designed to facilitate the collection and processing of the artefacts generated by the Sysdiagnose Analysis Framework (SAF).

This addon provides a set of configurations and scripts to streamline data ingestion and ensure proper handling within the Splunk environment.
8109.displayName: EC DIGIT CSIRC Add-on for Sysdiagnose Analysis Framework (SAF)
8109.name: ec_digit_saf_ta

8112.description:
Glass is a Splunk-native archive and restore solution that helps organizations meet long-term data retention requirements for threat hunting and compliance while also improving Splunk performance. Delivered as a Splunk App, Glass integrates directly with Splunk’s indexing policies and archives data to any S3-compatible object store, including S3 Glacier.

Key Benefits:

Security: Enables threat hunting on historical log data, months or even years old, to uncover long-running or previously unnoticed malicious activity.

Compliance: Supports retention requirements for regulatory or internal policies without overburdening your Splunk infrastructure.

Performance: Improves overall Splunk performance by reducing index size through efficient log and metadata archiving.

Flexible Architecture: Built to support additional SIEM and storage platforms beyond Splunk
8112.name: glass_app

8114.description:
Niksms Alert Action for Splunk enables Splunk users to send real-time SMS alerts to one or more phone numbers through the Niksms messaging API.

It integrates seamlessly with Splunk’s Alert Actions, providing a configurable, Python 3–compatible way to trigger SMS messages automatically based on search results or thresholds.
8114.name: niksms

8115.description: The Splunk Add-on for BMC Truesight allows a Splunk to create event to BMC Truesight via custom alerting. You just need to fill in usually it contain $result.fieldname$
8115.displayName: Custom Alert notification to BMC Truesight
8115.name: splunk_bmc_truesight

8123.description: Splunk app to visualise workflow and audit activity logged by AI workflow automation platform n8n.
8123.name: app_for_n8n

8127.description: RSS Feeder for Splunk Fetch and index RSS/Atom feeds directly into Splunk. This modular input lets you monitor security advisories, news, blogs, or any other RSS source in near real‑time, with dashboards to visualize feed activity, top authors, tags, and more.
8127.name: rss_feeder_for_splunk

8129.description: The SOCRadar Incidents app integrates SOCRadar's threat intelligence platform with Splunk SOAR, enabling automatic ingestion of security alarms for streamlined incident response. The app provides smart deduplication, status tracking, and configurable polling to help security teams efficiently manage and respond to threats detected by SOCRadar.
8129.name: socradar_incidents

8133.description: The Splunk DBX Add-on for Athena provides the JDBC driver for a Amazon Athena. Drivers can be used by other Splunk apps such as Splunk DB Connect and enable you to connect to Amazon Athena.
8133.displayName: Splunk DBX Add-on for Athena JDBC
8133.name: Splunk_JDBC_athena

8165.description: ALTIA-CSIRT Fortinet is an application that automates the deployment of a comprehensive suite of security alerts specifically designed for monitoring events generated by Fortinet firewall infrastructures. It enables analysts to rapidly implement critical detections over FortiGate log data, improving visibility across network traffic, threat activity, and policy enforcement. This solution minimizes operational overhead, ensures consistent security posture across environments, and significantly enhances the organization’s capacity for real-time threat detection and incident response.
8165.name: altia_csirt_fortinet

8166.description: ALTIA-CSIRT Splunk is an application that automates the deployment of a specialized set of security alerts focused on protecting the Splunk platform itself. It provides continuous monitoring of authentication events, configuration changes, privilege escalations, and anomalous activities within the Splunk environment. By enabling rapid detection of potential misuse or compromise of the SIEM infrastructure, this solution safeguards the integrity, availability, and confidentiality of the organization’s core security analytics platform, reinforcing trust in Splunk as the backbone of security operations.
8166.name: altia_csirt_splunk

8167.description: ALTIA-CSIRT AWS is an application that automates the deployment of a tailored set of security alerts designed to monitor events from AWS CloudTrail, AWS CloudWatch, and AWS WAF. It provides real-time visibility into account activity, network behavior, and web application protection, enabling early detection of unauthorized access, policy misconfigurations, or exploitation attempts. By streamlining the implementation of critical detections across AWS environments, this solution enhances cloud security posture, reduces manual workload for analysts, and accelerates incident response in hybrid and cloud-native infrastructures.
8167.name: ALTIA_CSIRT_AWS

8168.description: ALTIA-CSIRT M365 is an application that automates the deployment of a comprehensive set of security alerts specifically engineered for monitoring events across Microsoft 365 and Intune environments. It delivers advanced visibility into user activity, authentication patterns, device compliance, and configuration changes within the Microsoft ecosystem. By enabling rapid detection of suspicious logins, privilege misuse, or policy deviations, this solution strengthens identity and device security, reduces response time to cloud-based threats, and ensures continuous protection of corporate assets within the Microsoft 365 and Intune platforms.
8168.name: ALTIA_CSIRT_M365

8169.description: ALTIA-CSIRT Linux is an application that automates the deployment of a curated set of security alerts designed for monitoring Linux endpoints and servers. It provides deep visibility into system activity, user authentication, privilege escalation attempts, and anomalous process behavior across critical infrastructures. By detecting early indicators of compromise, misconfigurations, or unauthorized access, this solution reinforces endpoint resilience, optimizes analyst efficiency, and enhances the organization’s overall capability to detect, contain, and respond to threats targeting Linux-based environments.
8169.name: ALTIA_CSIRT_Linux

8170.description: ALTIA-CSIRT Active Directory is an application that automates the deployment of a focused set of security alerts dedicated to monitoring Active Directory environments. It offers comprehensive visibility into authentication events, group policy modifications, privilege escalations, and potential domain compromise activities. By detecting anomalous behavior and unauthorized administrative actions in real time, this solution fortifies identity security, prevents lateral movement within the network, and strengthens the integrity and resilience of the organization’s core directory infrastructure.
8170.name: ALTIA_CSIRT_Active_Directory

8172.description: ALTIA-CSIRT Vault is an application that automates the deployment of a diverse collection of security alerts sourced from non-standard or less common log sources across the organization’s infrastructure. It centralizes detections from niche systems, third-party tools, and custom integrations, ensuring that even unconventional data streams contribute to the overall security posture. By consolidating these heterogeneous signals into a unified detection framework, this solution extends monitoring coverage, uncovers hidden attack surfaces, and enhances the organization’s threat detection and response capabilities beyond traditional log sources.
8172.name: ALTIA_CSIRT_Vault

8173.description: The Method Security Audit Ingestion Add On provides an out of the box mechanism to ingest Method's structured audit logging from the Method Platform's Audit APIs. This allows security teams to connect Method audit logs to their Splunk instance with minimal effort to provide total coverage.
8173.displayName: Method Security Audit Ingestion Add On
8173.name: method-security-audit-ingestion

8188.description: ALTIA-CSIRT Web is an application that automates the deployment of security detections specifically focused on web-facing components such as web applications, proxies, WAFs, and related perimeter systems. It consolidates alerts derived from HTTP traffic analysis, application-layer events, and web security controls into a unified detection framework. By integrating signals from reverse proxies, load balancers, API gateways, and web application firewalls, the solution ensures comprehensive visibility across the organization’s digital front end. This centralized approach strengthens detection of web-based threats including injection attempts, session hijacking, and exploitation of application vulnerabilities while enhancing correlation, response accuracy, and overall resilience of the organization’s external exposure.
8188.name: ALTIA_CSIRT_Web

8199.description: This TA allows to ingest and parse logs for ufdbguard https://www.urlfilterdb.com/products/ufdbguard.html
8199.name: TA-ufdbguard

8225.description:
The VulnCheck Exploit Intelligence App for Splunk empowers security teams to enrich CVE data with real-world exploitation intelligence directly inside Splunk. By integrating VulnCheck's comprehensive APIs, the app delivers enriched vulnerability insights, SBOM-driven risk analytics, and threat actor correlation, enabling customers to prioritize vulnerabilities based on active exploitation and real risk, not just static CVSS scores.

*NOTE: This app requires a PAID subscription to VulnCheck Vulnerability & Exploit Intelligence*
8225.displayName: VulnCheck Exploit Intelligence App for Splunk
8225.name: vulncheck_exploit_intelligence_app_for_splunk

8229.description: Modular Input for pulling Qualtrics audit logs via API
8229.name: qualtrics_audit

8234.description: Send Search Results as Excel is a Splunk alert action that sends search results as a single Excel (.xls) file attached to an email. It is a Python 3–compatible refresh of the legacy “Send results as Excel” alert action, with updated SMTP/SSL handling and support for modern Splunk Enterprise versions.
8234.name: send_search_results_as_excel

8240.description: The Bitbucket Add-on for Splunk integrates Bitbucket repository data into your Splunk environment, enabling powerful monitoring and analysis of your development workflows. This add-on utilizes the Bitbucket REST API to collect repository activities, code changes, pull requests, and other version control metrics for enhanced visibility into your development processes.
8240.name: bitbucket_addon_for_splunk

8246.description: ManageEngine Alert Action for Splunk is an alert action which creates Support ticket, when an alert is triggered in Splunk. The addon uses API key to communicate with ManageEngine Servicedesk Plus Platform which is a Support ticketing platform.
8246.name: TA-manageengine-alert-action

8247.description: Modular Input and Search Commands to subscribe to NATS and watch JetStream KV.
8247.name: nats

8253.description:
App Purpose

  TA-suhlabs-eMASS is a Splunk Technology Add-on that ingests Plan of Action & Milestones (POA&M) data from the Enterprise Mission Assurance Support Service (eMASS) API into Splunk.

  Problem/Situation Addressed

  Organizations using eMASS for cybersecurity compliance and risk management face several challenges:

  Data Silos: Security compliance data resides in eMASS while operational security data lives in Splunk, creating fragmented visibility across the security program.

  Manual Reporting: Security teams must manually extract POA&M data from eMASS to create reports, analyze trends, or correlate vulnerabilities with other security events - a time-consuming and error-prone process.

  Limited Analytics: eMASS provides compliance tracking, but lacks the advanced analytics, correlation, and visualization capabilities needed for comprehensive security operations and risk analysis.

  Compliance Monitoring: Organizations need real-time monitoring of their security posture and remediation progress, but accessing this data requires switching between multiple systems.
8253.name: TA-suhlabs-eMASS

8254.description: The LinkShadow NDR Syslog App provides seamless ingestion, parsing, and normalization of LinkShadow's Network Detection & Response (NDR) CEF syslog events into Splunk. This app addresses the common challenge of standardizing and extracting actionable fields from LinkShadow alerts, allowing security teams to quickly analyze alerts, monitor threats, and correlate events in real time.
8254.name: TA-LinkShadowNDR

8257.description: Cloudflare Status keeps Splunk up to date with Cloudflare’s current status and incidents so teams can spot platform issues faster and accurately. It polls the public status API on your schedule writes clean JSON events with both live status and incident details. Drop it in, set the interval and index, and you’re ready to monitor.
8257.name: cloudflare_status

8260.description:
Integrating ANY.RUN’s solutions with Splunk platform is seamless with the ANY.RUN Add-on for Splunk. Easily connect your Spunk platform with all three flagship products by ANY.RUN – Interactive Sandbox, Threat Intelligence Lookup (TI Lookup), and Threat Intelligence Feeds (TI Feeds) – for faster investigations and response.
The app unlocks access to real-time updates, quick verdicts, and actionable statistics directly in Splunk, empowering analysts with automated enrichment and instant visibility into threats.

Use cases:
– Quick Verification: Analyze suspicious URLs from Spunk events in ANY.RUN Sandbox for rapid verdicts. Analysis results are stored as new Splunk events, with extracted IOCs captured for proactive detection of similar threats.
– Instant Enrichment: Enrich IOCs (hashes, IPs, domains, URLs) with threat context via TI Lookup. Tags, industry insights, and linked sandbox analyses will be automatically imported to Splunk.
– Real-Time Threat Data Import: Automated delivery of IOCs from TI Feeds to Splunk uncovers latest data on threats investigated by 15,000+ SOC teams worldwide.

Benefits:
– Faster Decision-Making: Accelerate MTTR with immediate verdicts and actionable insights.
– No Blind Spots: Contextualize alerts with fresh threat data for confident decisions.
– Verified Data: Near-zero false positives rate to save time and reduce workload.
– Deep Visibility: From high-level insights to detailed technical details, the app ensures visibility at all depth levels.
8260.name: TA-anyrun

8267.description: This application pulls various data from the Bitdefender GravityZone platform. For example, it can pull user activity logs or incidents.
8267.name: TA-bitdefender-gravityzone-data-collector

8269.description: A UCC modular input to poll for CriticalArc SafeZone audit logs via API
8269.name: safezone_audit

8275.description: Gemini Threat Hunter operationalizes Generative AI in Splunk by integrating Google Gemini 2.5 models. It automates log analysis, provides clear verdicts (True/False Positive) with reasoning, and reduces alert fatigue for SOC analysts.
8275.name: TA-gemini-splunk

8278.description: Agent management versioned app retrieval add-on fetches Splunk apps and configurations from configured artifact repositories, such as GitHub, GitLab, JFrog Artifactory, and Sonatype Nexus Repository, for deployment by agent management.
8278.name: agent_management_versioned_app_retrieval

8279.description:
Sectigo simplifies your security operations by providing secure access control to retrieve audit logs directly within your Splunk environment, including Splunk Enterprise and Splunk Cloud.

On average, users with a centralized log base experience a notable decrease in security incidents, due to enhanced monitoring of security-related events.
8279.name: TA-sectigo-audit

8280.description:
The Technology Add-on for macOS Endpoint Logs (TA-macOS) provides index-time and search-time configurations for collecting and normalizing endpoint logs from macOS systems that have the Splunk Universal Forwarder installed. It focuses on native macOS logs, specifically `/var/log/system.log` and `/var/log/install.log`, and turns them into analytics-ready data for security and operations use cases.

This add-on defines consistent sourcetypes, handles multiline events correctly, extracts core fields, and provides CIM-friendly eventtypes and tags that support Splunk Enterprise Security data models such as Authentication, Change, and Endpoint.
8280.name: TA-macOS

8283.description:
This is a Splunk Technology Add-on (TA) that collects data from Palo Alto Networks devices via API and ingests it into Splunk. It enables ingestion of logs and telemetry from Palo Alto Networks devices via API (instead of just syslog/HEC).

 It is designed to complement existing log-based data collection by providing a flexible, API-driven method — ideal for environments where direct syslog forwarding is not feasible, where firewalls are behind NAT or restricted networks, or where additional metadata must be collected beyond traditional log streams.

Users can create their own custom API endpoint scripts that require additional-parsing or custom logic with this add-on.
8283.displayName: Palo Alto API Inputs Add On
8283.name: palo_alto_addon_for_splunk

8284.description: This is for the normalization for Sangfor Network Secure Firewall logs.
8284.name: TA-nsf-sangfor

8285.description:
The Cribl Decrypt Add-On for Splunk provides a custom command - cribldecryptv2 - that will decrypt encrypted data ingested from Cribl at search time.

Note: Version 2.0.0 and above of this command only works on Linux Search Heads. It will not work on Windows because Splunk does not ship the required Windows Python library.
8285.name: TA-cribl-decrypt

8290.description:
IBM Guardium REST API Add-on for Splunk enables secure, automated ingestion of database activity and audit logs from IBM Guardium into Splunk using the Guardium online_report REST API.

This add-on provides a fully checkpointed, fault-tolerant modular input that continuously pulls SQL activity data at a configurable interval and resumes seamlessly after restarts without duplicating events. It supports per-input Guardium API URLs, central token-based authentication, optional SSL verification, and scalable pagination for high-volume environments.

All ingested events are structured as JSON and can be easily normalized to the Splunk Common Information Model (CIM) Database data model for use with Enterprise Security, custom dashboards, and compliance reporting.

This add-on is ideal for organizations that rely on IBM Guardium for database security monitoring and want centralized analytics, detection, and long-term retention in Splunk.
8290.name: TA-guardium

8292.description: This Splunk app simplifies the data onboarding process, especially for non-technical users. It allows users to fetch data from APIs and store it into either a Splunk KVStore or an Index, with an intuitive UI to preview, pre-process, and manage inputs.
8292.name: api-data-input

8293.description: Telegram App for Splunk is a custom alert action that sends Splunk correlation search results and alerts directly to Telegram – to private chats, group chats, or topics (threads) in supergroups. It supports direct connection or HTTP/HTTPS proxy.
8293.name: telegram_app

8297.description: Real-time monitoring application for Hydro-Quebec's electrical grid, providing complete visibility into outages, planned interruptions, and electricity consumption in province of Quebec, Canada.
8297.name: hydroquebec_pannes_et_interruptions

8299.description: VS Code Audit Add-on by zuykn.io - Collects Microsoft Visual Studio Code configuration, extensions, workspace settings, and remote development telemetry for security and operational monitoring.
8299.name: TA-vscode_audit

8305.description:
# Send dashboard PDF

## Overview
This TA includes a custom alert action that implements a dashboard PDF send over email functionality of any dashboard.
It is work in progress but it should work for classic simpleXML and Dashboard Studio.

## Release notes
- 1.0.1: Added Mail cc and Dashboard Studio support.
- 1.0.0: First release.
8305.displayName: Send PDF dashboard alert action add-on
8305.name: TA-send-dashboard-alert-action

8308.description:
GenAI Observability for Splunk provides comprehensive monitoring for LLM and GenAI applications. Track token usage, latency, costs, and errors across OpenAI, Anthropic, Langchain, and other AI providers.

Key Features:
- Real-time monitoring of LLM calls, RAG pipelines, and AI agents
- Token usage tracking with cost estimation
- Latency analysis with P50, P95, P99 percentiles
- Error detection and alerting
- Pre-built dashboards for immediate insights
- Python SDK for easy instrumentation
- Support for OpenAI, Anthropic, Langchain, and more

Use Cases:
- Monitor production AI applications
- Optimize token usage and reduce costs
- Debug slow or failing LLM calls
- Track AI spend by model, workflow, or user
- Ensure SLA compliance for AI services

This app includes ready-to-use dashboards, lookups with model pricing, and a lightweight Python SDK that instruments your code with simple decorators.
8308.name: splunk_genai_observability

8316.description:
The Data Dictionary App contains a script which adds an _meta field dd= for all included deployment apps in /etc/apps or /etc/system (defaults to all apps). The Data Dictionary App should be used in conjunction with the Data Dictionary Add-on to maintain an up to date Data Dictionary showing which input apps are sending through what data.

Please note:
•	DO NOT change the name of the app or any internal files.
•	This app/add-on will increase the size of .tsidx files and includes a dashboard containing an index=* search which defaults to the last 15min. BEWARE!
•	The script uses btool to generate a list of all the apps containing input.conf files. If you have an input stanza spread across 5 apps (for some reason) only one of those apps will be listed in your data dictionary. 

As of version 1.0.0: 
•	If you wish to change the frequency with which the script is run, you must do so inside the script.py file itself.
•	If you wish to change the list of excluded apps, you must do so inside the script.py file itself.

Please splunk responsibly.
8316.name: data_dic_App

8318.description: Monitor Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) alerts with automated MITRE ATT&CK threat intelligence enrichment. This application collects alerts every 15 minutes, maps 185+ MITRE techniques, calculates threat scores (0-100). Perfect for SOC teams and threat intelligence analysts monitoring Canadian cybersecurity threats.
8318.name: cccs_alerts_app

8320.description:
Hitachi Content Platform (HCP Classic v10.0.0.131) exposes operational and performance metrics through built-in Prometheus endpoints. Without Splunk integration, administrators must manually troubleshoot storage, gateway, and replication issues.

Hitachi HCP Classic for Splunk automatically collects Prometheus metrics from HCP over secure HTTPS and indexes them into Splunk as structured events. This allows Splunk users to monitor system health, API latency, storage usage, replication status, and service activity from a single place.

The add-on supports both collect-all metrics and selective metric collection, and works with HCP’s documented Prometheus interface on port 9094.
8320.name: TA-hitachi-hcp-classic-for-splunk

8323.description:
The Dropzone AI Add-on for Splunk integrates Dropzone AI's security automation platform with Splunk Enterprise, enabling security operations teams to monitor platform health and analyze investigation data directly within their Splunk environment.

Security operations teams face challenges with fragmented security tools and manual investigation processes that slow down incident response. Dropzone AI addresses these issues by providing AI-driven security automation that streamlines triage, enrichment, and response workflows. This add-on bridges the gap between Dropzone AI's automation capabilities and Splunk's powerful analytics and visualization platform.

Key capabilities include:

• Health Monitoring: Continuously monitors Dropzone AI instance availability and connectivity, providing real-time visibility into platform status
• Investigation Data Collection: Automatically ingests completed investigation data including conclusions, attack surfaces, alert types, and executive summaries
• Integrated Dashboards: Pre-built dashboards visualize health status, investigation trends, and security insights alongside your existing Splunk data
• Unified Analysis: Enables correlation of Dropzone AI investigation data with other security events in Splunk for comprehensive threat analysis
• Automated Workflows: Supports integration of Dropzone AI's AI-driven security automation into your Splunk-based security operations workflows

This integration helps security teams eliminate manual toil, accelerate decision-making, and gain deeper insights by combining Dropzone AI's automation capabilities with Splunk's powerful search, analytics, and visualization features.
8323.name: dropzone_add_on

8327.description:
xPi-Hole DNS and Ad-Blocker Analytics transforms your Pi-Hole DNS data into actionable insights with enterprise-grade analytics and visualizations.

  Key Features:
  - 6 production-ready dashboards with 52+ visualizations
  - Real-time DNS query and ad blocking analytics
  - Per-client behavioral analysis and performance metrics
  - Geographic destination mapping with choropleth and marker maps
  - Full Splunk CIM and OCSF compliance for Enterprise Security integration
  - 27 event types for granular searching and alerting
  - UI-based configuration - no file editing required

  Monitoring Capabilities:
  - DNS query volume, types, and patterns
  - Ad blocking effectiveness and block source analysis
  - Cache hit rates and performance metrics
  - Client device activity and behavior
  - Geographic distribution of DNS resolutions
  - Rate limiting and security events

  Supported Data:
  - Pi-Hole DNS logs (dnsmasq)
  - FTL daemon logs
  - Gravity update logs
  - Web interface access logs

  Requirements:
  - Splunk Enterprise 8.0+ or Splunk Cloud
  - Pi-Hole v5.0+

  Developed by xTechnix Inc. | https://xtechnix.com
8327.name: xpihole

8329.description:
The Radware DefensePro & VRM Integration App for Splunk provides real-time and historical visibility into DDoS mitigation and traffic behavior across Radware DefensePro devices managed by Radware Cyber Controller.

This app solves the challenge of monitoring mitigation effectiveness and attack impact by collecting, normalizing, and indexing key DefensePro metrics directly into Splunk. It enables security and network teams to analyze mitigation bandwidth, traffic rates, and attack categories across single or multiple DefensePro devices from a centralized Splunk interface.

By leveraging Radware CC APIs, the app dynamically discovers DefensePro devices and continuously ingests:

Mitigation bandwidth per attack category (e.g., Behavioral DoS, Anomalies)

Traffic statistics and rates per DefensePro

Time-based metrics suitable for dashboards, alerts, and forensic analysis

The app empowers SOC, NOC, and DDoS response teams to:

Visualize mitigation bandwidth trends over time

Compare attack impact across DefensePro devices

Identify dominant attack categories during mitigation events

Correlate Radware mitigation data with other security and network telemetry in Splunk

This integration reduces operational blind spots, eliminates manual data extraction from CC, and provides a scalable analytics foundation for proactive DDoS monitoring and incident response.
8329.name: TA-radware_traffic

833.description:
*** Important: Read upgrade Instructions and test add-on update before deploying to production ***
There are changes to default indexes and .conf changes in version 6.0 of Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux that can break an existing installation if upgrade instructions are not followed in detail. If an existing Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux is being upgraded, please test in a non-production environment first.


The Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux works with the Splunk App for Unix and Linux to provide rapid insights and operational visibility into large-scale Unix and Linux environments.  With its new pre-packaged alerting capability, flexible service-based hosts grouping, and easy management of many data sources, it arms administrators with a powerful ability to quickly identify performance and capacity bottlenecks and outliers in Unix and Linux environments.
833.displayName: Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux
833.name: Splunk_TA_nix

8331.description:
The Zeroception-Pulse Threat-Intel app transforms threat intelligence operations by providing automated collection and comprehensive analysis of cyber threat indicators directly within your Splunk environment. This Technology Add-on addresses the critical challenge faced by security teams who need real-time, actionable threat intelligence integrated seamlessly into their existing SIEM workflows.

Problem Statement:
Security operations centers struggle with fragmented threat intelligence sources, manual indicator lookups, and lack of centralized threat visibility. Traditional approaches require analysts to pivot between multiple platforms, leading to delayed response times and missed threat correlations. Organizations need a unified solution that automatically ingests, normalizes, and visualizes threat data within their Splunk infrastructure.

Solution:
Our app delivers enterprise-grade threat intelligence automation through:

Comprehensive Indicator Coverage: Automatically collects multiple critical indicator types including network indicators, vulnerabilities, file signatures, and communication vectors from the Zeroception Pulse API

Real-Time Intelligence Dashboards: Purpose-built interactive dashboards providing instant threat visibility, from executive overviews to deep technical analysis

Intelligent Search Capabilities: Auto-detection technology that identifies indicator types and provides unified search across all threat data

Advanced Analytics: Geographic threat mapping, malware family analysis, infrastructure clustering, and vulnerability tracking with scoring

Enterprise Security Features: Full Traffic Light Protocol (TLP) classification, confidence scoring, and role-based access controls

Key Benefits:

Accelerated Response Times: Eliminate manual threat lookups with automated collection intervals

Enhanced Threat Visibility: Centralized threat intelligence within existing Splunk workflows

Improved Decision Making: Executive dashboards and trend analysis for strategic security planning

Compliance Ready: AppInspect certified with comprehensive audit logging and data retention policies

Zero Maintenance: Automated collection with built-in error handling and health monitoring

This app is ideal for organizations seeking to enhance their threat detection capabilities, streamline security operations, and gain comprehensive visibility into the global threat landscape without leaving their Splunk ecosystem.
8331.name: zeroception-threat-intel

8334.description:
The Border Add-on for OutSystems Logs enables seamless integration between OutSystems Platform and Splunk. 

This add-on collects logs from the OutSystems Monitor Probe API, supporting all 8 endpoint types: Errors, Extensions, General, Integrations, Mobile Requests, Request Events, Timers, and Web Requests.

Key Features:
• Automatic checkpointing - resumes from last position after restarts
• Built-in deduplication - prevents duplicate events using configurable key fields
• Time-chunked fetching - efficiently handles large time ranges
• Event delay support - ensures all logs are available before collection
• Splunk Cloud compatible - uses KV Store for state management
• GUI-based configuration - no manual config file editing required

The add-on handles API rate limiting, SSL verification options, and provides detailed logging for troubleshooting.
8334.name: Border_Add_on_for_OutSystems_Logs

8335.description: Custom visualization for rendering Directed Acyclic Graphs (DAGs) using GraphViz. Supports raw DOT format and structured source/target data for attack trees, workflows, dependencies, and network topologies.
8335.name: viz_dag_viewer

8336.description:
Elevate your Splunk Dashboards with Premium, Professional Themes.

The Splunk Theme Manager is a powerful application designed to transform standard Splunk dashboards into visually stunning, professional interfaces. With a curated collection of high-quality themes with wide range of themes, this app allows you to apply complex CSS styling with a single click—no coding required.

🎨 Premium Theme Gallery: Browse a vast collection of professionally designed themes, categorized by style (Modern, Vibrant, Professional, Nature, etc.).
⚡ CSS-Only Architecture: All themes are built using pure CSS/XML, ensuring zero-javascript execution for maximum security and performance.
👁️ Live Preview: Instantly preview how a theme will look before applying it to your dashboard.
🌓 Dark & Light Modes: Full support for both dark and light dashboard modes, with themes optimized for specific lighting conditions.
💧 Custom Watermarks: Add professional branding to your dashboards with customizable SVG watermarks (supports Animations, Base64 encoding).
📄 Pagination & Organization: efficiently browse through the extensive theme library with built-in pagination and filtering.
🔧 Zero Maintenance: "Set it and forget it" styling that applies strictly via standard Splunk Simple XML extensions.
8336.name: dashboard_theme_manager

8337.description: The Ransomware Add-on for Splunk integrates ransomware-related threat intelligence and incident data into your Splunk environment. This add-on leverages Python scripts to collect, parse, and organize data such as victim details, indicators of compromise (IOCs), ransomware notes, negotiation information, and YARA rules. It provides enhanced visibility into ransomware activities and enables efficient monitoring, alerting, and analysis within your Splunk ecosystem
8337.name: ransomware_addon_for_splunk

8341.description:
Purpose:
Crypto Firewall for Splunk provides a curated, continuously maintained lookup of known malicious cryptocurrency-related IP addresses for use in Splunk searches, alerts, and dashboards.

Problem addressed:
Cryptocurrency-related abuse—including scams, malware infrastructure, illicit mining, fraud endpoints, and command-and-control servers—often bypasses traditional security controls. Security teams need a lightweight, transparent way to enrich logs with crypto-specific threat intelligence inside Splunk.

What the app does:
This app installs the Crypto Firewall CSV as a Splunk lookup table, enabling users to quickly identify and correlate events involving known malicious crypto infrastructure. The lookup can be used across network, proxy, DNS, firewall, and application logs to:

- Detect connections to known malicious crypto IPs

- Enrich events with clear threat context

- Power alerts, dashboards, and investigations

- Reduce time to detection for crypto-related threats

Key characteristics:

- Uses a simple CSV lookup compatible with native Splunk lookups

- No external dependencies or binaries

- Designed for Splunk Enterprise and Splunk Cloud

- Transparent, auditable threat data source

- Suitable for security monitoring, threat hunting, and SOC workflows

This app is intentionally minimal and focused, allowing organisations to integrate crypto-focused threat intelligence into existing Splunk workflows without complexity or performance overhead.
8341.name: crypto_firewall

8349.description:
The Nutanix Prism Central TA for Splunk Enterprise empowers Nutanix Prism Central customers to seamlessly ingest data from Nutanix Prism Central Rest API. This integration offers you valuable insights into their virtual environments, which are centrally managed by Nutanix Prism Central.

For detailed information, please refer Nutanix Prism Central Splunk  application user guide at https://portal.nutanix.com/page/documents/details?targetId=NTNX-prism-central-splunk-application-user-guide:NTNX-prism-central-splunk-application-user-guide
8349.name: TA-nutanix-PC

8351.description:
The Trellix (McAfee) ePO Splunk Technology Add-on enables Splunk users to reliably collect, normalize, and analyze security telemetry from Trellix ePolicy Orchestrator (ePO) in one centralized platform. Many organizations running Trellix ePO lack a native, CIM-compliant integration with Splunk, making it difficult to correlate endpoint security data with other security and IT signals.

This app addresses that gap by providing a production-ready integration that ingests threat events, malware detections, endpoint and agent health, policy compliance, quarantine activity, updates, and user audit logs via the ePO REST API (and syslog where applicable). All data is normalized to the Splunk Common Information Model (CIM), allowing immediate use with Splunk Enterprise Security, Security Essentials, and custom SOC workflows.

By combining secure data collection, enterprise-grade reliability, and a comprehensive all-in-one security dashboard, the add-on helps SOC teams, security engineers, and Splunk administrators gain clear visibility into endpoint threats, compliance posture, and operational health—without building and maintaining custom integrations.

Note: This is a community-maintained, non-official add-on. It is not affiliated with Splunk or Trellix. "/sarat1kyan/TA-trellix-epo"
8351.name: TA-trellix-epo

8355.description:
The Nutanix Prism Central App for Splunk allows customers of Splunk Enterprise and Nutanix to visualize and view the state of their Prism Central from Splunk. In addition, the app allows you to view log events surrounding all Nutanix processes and search specific Nutanix syslog data within the app.

For detailed information, please refer Nutanix Prism Central Splunk  application user guide at https://portal.nutanix.com/page/documents/details?targetId=NTNX-prism-central-splunk-application-user-guide:NTNX-prism-central-splunk-application-user-guide
8355.displayName: Nutanix Prism Central Dashboard for Splunk
8355.name: splunk-app-for-nutanix-PC

8358.description:
The Webex Alert app enables Splunk administrators to send automated alert notifications directly to Cisco Webex Teams rooms. This integration allows security operations centers (SOCs) and IT teams to receive real-time alerts from Splunk searches and correlations in their collaborative Webex spaces, ensuring immediate visibility and rapid response to critical events.

Key Features:
- Send Splunk alert results to any Webex Teams room or space
- Support for both individual and group/team spaces
- Customize message formatting with alert details
- Include search results, field values, and metadata in notifications
- Secure authentication using Webex Bot tokens
- Easy configuration through Splunk's alert actions interface
- Compatible with scheduled searches, real-time alerts, and ad-hoc searches

Use Cases:

Security incident notifications for SOC teams
Infrastructure monitoring alerts for DevOps teams
Compliance and audit notifications
Business metrics and KPI alerts
Application performance monitoring
Custom alert workflows for any Splunk use case

This app extends Splunk's native alerting capabilities by leveraging Webex Teams as a modern, collaborative notification channel, enabling teams to discuss and act on alerts in real-time within their existing communication workflows.
8358.name: webex_alert

8363.description:
This Splunk add-on is designed to collect and ingest operational insights data from Cisco’s Business Critical Services (BCS) API into Splunk. It enables users to automatically fetch a wide range of detailed device, inventory, configuration, risk mitigation, and product alert information from Cisco’s API endpoints and index this data within Splunk for analysis and monitoring.

The app addresses the challenge of integrating Cisco BCS operational data into Splunk by providing a modular input that handles authentication, API rate limiting, pagination, and event creation for multiple data sources. This allows customers—particularly those with Cisco Advanced Services—to gain centralized visibility into their Cisco device inventory, configuration best practices, security advisories, hardware and software lifecycle alerts, and other critical operational metrics without manual data collection.

By automating the retrieval and indexing of this comprehensive Cisco operational data, the app helps organizations improve their network management, risk assessment, and compliance monitoring capabilities within the Splunk platform.
8363.name: TA-lcs-plug-in

8364.description:
Ops Pulse is a lightweight operational intelligence app that provides instant health indicators and anomaly detection for any existing Splunk data.

The app works with any index and any log type, requiring no external integrations, agents, or paid Splunk add-ons. It helps teams quickly understand whether their systems are behaving normally, detect error spikes, volume anomalies, and identify early signs of degradation.

Key capabilities

> Index-agnostic operational health dashboards

> Error rate and volume trend analysis

> Baseline-based anomaly detection

> Early warning indicators for slow degradation

> Simple, explainable health score

> Works out of the box with existing Splunk data
8364.name: ops_pulse

8365.description: This Add-on allows users to pull data from the Webex REST API to Splunk.
8365.name: ta_cisco_webex_add_on_for_splunk

8368.description:
DFIRVault is an advanced, analyst-driven threat detection and investigation platform built entirely in Splunk. It combines detection engineering, behavior analytics, and investigation workflows into a single operational view for security operations and digital forensics teams.

The app ingests Windows security, Sysmon, PowerShell, and endpoint telemetry and automatically enriches events with MITRE ATT&CK techniques, risk scores, kill-chain stages, adversary emulation context, and entity relationships.

DFIRVault goes beyond traditional dashboards by incorporating:

- Risk-based alerting (RBA)
- Peer group and golden image baselining
- Attack path and blast radius inference
- Entity-centric timelines
- MITRE coverage analysis and detection gap identification
- Integrated investigation notebooking

This allows analysts to move seamlessly from detection → triage → scoping → investigation → documentation, all without leaving Splunk.

DFIRVault is designed to be modular, transparent, and extensible — making it suitable for blue teams, DFIR responders, threat hunters, and detection engineers alike.
8368.name: DFIRDashboards

8374.description:
OAI (Observalytics AI) integrates your local Ollama instance directly into Splunk, enabling AI-powered analysis without sending data to external services.

The Problem: Security and operations teams want AI-driven insights but face compliance, privacy, and cost concerns with cloud-based AI APIs. Sending log data to external services introduces risk and may violate data governance policies.

The Solution: OAI connects Splunk to your local Ollama server, keeping all inference on-premises. Your data never leaves your environment — no API keys, no cloud dependencies, no per-token costs.

Key Benefits:
- Privacy-first: All inference happens locally — your data never leaves your environment
- Simple syntax: Just `| oai "your question"` — intuitive SPL integration
- Index investigation: Automatically analyze any index for volume, EPS, log types, and anomalies
- Event classification: Pipe events into OAI to have the model categorize and explain them
- Fully configurable: Choose your model, endpoint, timeouts, and more via the setup page

Perfect for security analysts, SOC teams, and anyone who wants to augment their Splunk workflows with AI-driven analysis while maintaining complete data sovereignty.
8374.name: oai

8377.description: Technology Add-on for ingesting and analyzing Model Context Protocol (MCP) JSON-RPC messages from MCP servers. Provides standardized field extractions, CIM compliance, and pre-configured sourcetype for monitoring AI assistant tool usage including file operations, API calls,  GitHub activity and more.
8377.name: TA-mcp-jsonrpc

8380.description: OnStak - Smart Data Tiering helps Splunk administrators and data teams to scan incoming datasets, identifies key infrastructure KPIs, and recommends appropriate tiering—specifically prompting users to store high-value numerical indicators (KPIs) such as those from machines and containers in metrics format, while less-performant or diagnostic data remains in log format. The tool is designed to enforce industry best practices around data storage and observability, maximizing Splunk efficiency and value.
8380.name: onstak_metrics_accelerator

8382.description:
**Airia Platform Analytics** provides enterprise-grade monitoring and analytics for AI platform operations, addressing the critical challenge of managing costs, security, and performance across multiple AI providers.

The Airia Platform Analytics app for Splunk transforms raw AI platform logs into actionable intelligence through comprehensive monitoring dashboards and automated analytics.

**Real-Time Cost Tracking** - Monitor spending across 14+ AI models with automatic cost calculation per request. Identify cost trends by model, user, prompt, and time period to stay within budget and optimize spending.

**Security & Compliance** - Track DLP policy violations in real-time with dedicated security dashboards, ensuring sensitive data protection and regulatory compliance.

**Performance Analytics** - Measure execution times, success rates, token usage, and model performance to optimize application responsiveness and identify bottlenecks.

**Executive Insights** - High-level KPIs, trends, and usage patterns enable data-driven decision making for leadership teams.

## Key Features

- **5 Pre-Built Dashboards**: Cost, Performance, Usage, Executive, and DLP monitoring with interactive drill-down capabilities
- **40+ Automated Field Extractions**: Model details, costs, tokens, performance metrics, and user data automatically extracted from JSON logs
- **Accelerated Data Models**: Optimized for fast analytics queries on high-cardinality AI telemetry data
- **Intelligent Lookups**: Pre-configured pricing tables for 14 AI models and completion status mappings
- **Dedicated Index**: Optimized for nested JSON AI logs with configurable retention and sizing

## Use Cases

- DevOps teams monitoring AI application health and performance
- Finance teams tracking and forecasting AI infrastructure costs
- Security teams detecting and investigating DLP violations
- Engineering teams optimizing model selection and prompt efficiency
- Leadership teams understanding AI platform ROI and usage trends

## Quick Start

Install the app, configure syslog inputs (TCP/UDP port 514), point your Airia Platform logs to Splunk, and immediately gain visibility into your AI operations through pre-built dashboards and automated field extractions.
8382.name: splunk-app-airia

8387.description:
SplunkLint is an advanced dashboard analysis and optimization toolkit designed to solve the lack of deep validation, performance insight, and maintainability tooling in native Splunk. While Splunk allows users to build dashboards, it provides limited feedback on XML correctness, SPL performance, token usage, accessibility, and long-term scalability. SplunkLint addresses this gap by giving dashboard developers, admins, and architects a single place to validate, analyze, optimize, compare, document, and modernize Splunk dashboards.

The app helps teams prevent broken or inefficient dashboards, identify performance bottlenecks before they impact users, detect security and best-practice issues, understand token flows, migrate Simple XML dashboards to Dashboard Studio, and generate clear documentation automatically. In short, SplunkLint turns dashboards from “working” into production-ready, optimized, accessible, and maintainable assets across Splunk Enterprise and Splunk Cloud.
8387.name: TA-splunklint

8405.description: Splunk Native Embedder enables native dashboard embedding using Splunk’s ?embed=1 mode—no proxy, no custom authentication layer. It provides a simple UI to manage the embedding headers (x_frame_options_sameorigin, iframe allowances) and cookie behavior (cookieSameSite, tools.sessions.secure) needed for cross‑site embedding. The app also keeps a legacy, no‑op modular input (embedder://) to preserve compatibility for older installs. This solves the common problem of “Splunk won’t load in an iframe” and “cookies are blocked in embedded logins” while keeping a clean, native deployment.
8405.name: splunk_native_embedder

8413.description: This is a Supporting App designed to be deployed alongside Sideview's commercial "Cisco CDR Reporting and Analytics" app.  It provides the Splunk configuration to ingest the CDR data from Cisco Meeting Server. Additionally it contains various pieces of Splunk configuration and logic that are used by Cisco CDR Reporting and Analytics, to make the CMS CDR work within its user interfaces.
8413.displayName: Supporting App for Cisco Meeting Server
8413.name: SA_cisco_meeting_server

8414.description:
Service Reliability Signals is a lightweight, integration-free Splunk app that transforms existing application and service logs into actionable reliability and availability insights.
The app detects service instability, short outages, SLA risk, and recurring incident patterns using only log data - no agents, OS metrics, or premium Splunk add-ons required.
8414.name: service_reliability_signals

8416.description:
Alpha Level automatically reduces security alert fatigue by identifying false positive and duplicate alerts, allowing your SOC team to focus on genuine threats.

THE PROBLEM
Security teams are overwhelmed by alert volume. Studies show up to 80% of security alerts are false positives or duplicates, leading to analyst burnout, missed threats, and wasted resources.

HOW IT WORKS
Alpha Level uses two ML-powered detection methods:

  • Regularity Detection - Identifies alerts that exhibit predictable patterns typically associated with benign automated activity.

  • Duplication Detection - Identifies alerts that are semantically similar to recent alerts, reducing noise from alert storms.

KEY FEATURES
  • Automatic enrichment of Splunk ES notable events
  • Alpha Score (0-100) indicates likelihood of true positive
  • Works out of the box with no tuning required
  • Supports custom alert sources beyond Splunk ES
  • Simple Overview dashboard for monitoring

REQUIREMENTS
  • Splunk Enterprise 8.2+
  • Python for Scientific Computing (PSC) add-on for your platform
  • Splunk Enterprise Security (optional, recommended)

Alpha Level integrates seamlessly with your existing Splunk ES workflow, adding actionable context to every alert.
8416.name: TA-alpha-level-linux

8417.description:
Alpha Level automatically reduces security alert fatigue by identifying false positive and duplicate alerts, allowing your SOC team to focus on genuine threats.

THE PROBLEM
Security teams are overwhelmed by alert volume. Studies show up to 80% of security alerts are false positives or duplicates, leading to analyst burnout, missed threats, and wasted resources.

HOW IT WORKS
Alpha Level uses two ML-powered detection methods:

  • Regularity Detection - Identifies alerts that exhibit predictable patterns typically associated with benign automated activity.

  • Duplication Detection - Identifies alerts that are semantically similar to recent alerts, reducing noise from alert storms.

KEY FEATURES
  • Automatic enrichment of Splunk ES notable events
  • Alpha Score (0-100) indicates likelihood of true positive
  • Works out of the box with no tuning required
  • Supports custom alert sources beyond Splunk ES
  • Simple Overview dashboard for monitoring

REQUIREMENTS
  • Splunk Enterprise 8.2+
  • Python for Scientific Computing (PSC) add-on for your platform
  • Splunk Enterprise Security (optional, recommended)

Alpha Level integrates seamlessly with your existing Splunk ES workflow, adding actionable context to every alert.
8417.name: TA-alpha-level-windows

8420.description:
PROBLEM: Within Splunk architectures that utilize frozen storage of data ingested into Splunk, the data then accumulates until space runs out and mechanisms outside of Splunk itself will need to be used to remain vigilant of the data age and volume.
SOLUTION: Subzero aims to provide a user interface to quickly identify the age of frozen data so that the administrator can take appropriate actions such as thaw out or delete specific data.
8420.name: subzero

8423.description: Vercel Drains Add-on provides CIM-aligned field extractions for Vercel Logs, Speed Insights, and Web Analytics ingested via Splunk HTTP Event Collector (HEC). It normalizes common web, performance, and change fields (src_ip, status, url, response_time, lcp/cls/inp, change_id), supports schema‑based routing, and includes sample events and a validation test plan. This add-on does not create inputs or manage HEC tokens; it focuses on props/transforms and search‑time normalization for Splunk Cloud‑friendly deployments.
8423.name: splunk_ta_vercel

8424.description: Vercel Observability provides dashboards and alerts for frontend performance, deployment health, and edge security using Vercel Drains data ingested via Splunk HEC. It visualizes Core Web Vitals (LCP/CLS/INP), deployment‑level error trends, and traffic anomalies, and includes baseline alerts for regressions and spikes. This app expects the Vercel Drains Add‑on (splunk_ta_vercel) to be installed for field extractions and CIM‑aligned normalization.
8424.name: splunk_app_vercel

8429.description: This Supporting App adapts data indexed via Splunk's "Cisco Webex Add-on for Splunk" allowing users to leverage that data within Sideview's commercial app "Cisco CDR Reporting and Analytics".
8429.name: SA_sideview_webex

8431.description:
OpenCTI is an open-source platform developed by Filigran to help organizations manage their cyber threat intelligence and observables. It enables cybersecurity teams to efficiently organize, store, and operationalize threat information across technical, operational, and strategic levels.

OpenCTI for Splunk Enterprise enables Splunk users to interconnect Splunk with OpenCTI and to leverage threat information to improve detection capabilities and response to security incidents.

This new application represents a major evolution from the previous version (OpenCTI Add-On) and stands out through:
- The ability to import multiple object types, not just indicators
- An optimized architecture for clustered Splunk environments and Splunk Cloud, enabling the feeding of a Splunk Index and the use of saved searches to populate KV Stores
- A migration to the UCC (Universal Configuration Console) framework that implements best practices in development

Key features:
- Modular inputs for ingesting OpenCTI data via the OpenCTI Stream API
- Ability to trigger OpenCTI actions in response to Alerts and to investigate them directly in OpenCTI
-Support for multiple object types (Indicators, Observables, Relationships, Sightings)
About Filigran
Filigran (www.filigran.io ) provides open-source cybersecurity solutions covering threat intelligence management, breach and attack simulation, and cyber risk management.
8431.name: TA-opencti-for-splunk-enterprise

8438.description:
The InSitzes App for Splunk Enterprise is a unified monitoring suite that gives administrators actionable insight into ingestion, system performance, workload management, search efficiency, and compute consumption across their Splunk Enterprise deployment. Rather than stitching together the Monitoring Console, license pages, introspection data, and ad-hoc searches, admins get one React-powered dashboard with purpose-built tabs to answer the questions that matter most: Is my environment healthy? Is data flowing reliably? Are searches running efficiently? Am I approaching my license quota? And what would my workload cost under Splunk Cloud compute-based licensing?

The Health tab runs 31 automated checks across 8 categories — System, Ingestion, Data Quality, Search, Compute, Workload, Forwarding, and Capacity — with weighted severity scoring and a rolled-up environment health score so you know immediately where to focus. Individual tabs drill into CPU, memory, and disk utilization across every server role (search heads, indexers, cluster manager, deployment server, SHC deployer); live license usage with 7-day trend against daily quota; forwarder throughput, HEC health, SSL and deployment-client issues; sourcetype parsing, timestamp, and debug-event quality; scheduled search execution, skip reasons, and wasteful-search detection; WLM filtered, reclassified, and aborted search rates; and dashboard refresh frequency with base vs. chained search analysis.

The Compute tab delivers an hourly-computed SVC estimate — via the bundled insitzes_svc_estimation saved search and dynamic role discovery on _introspection data — so Enterprise admins can see exactly what their workload would cost under Splunk Cloud compute-based licensing, broken down by search head, app, search type, user, and provenance, with an optional Cost Mode. Install on your Monitoring Console search head; ingestion data is read live from _internal license_usage events, eliminating scheduled-lookup maintenance. Pre-built alert saved searches (disabled by default) cover ingestion anomalies, app updates, large lookup files, and redundant scheduled searches.
8438.displayName: InSitzes App for Splunk Enterprise Monitoring
8438.name: insitzes_app_for_splunk_enterprise

8450.description:
The Userful Infinity app provides a secure, enterprise-grade bridge between Splunk’s data visualization capabilities and the Userful Infinity operational platform. It enables organizations to project real-time Splunk dashboards onto mission-critical video walls and distributed displays without the security risks of exposed credentials or manual login prompts.

It provides a secure gateway that enables seamless dashboard embedding into the Userful Infinity platform using SSO header injection and IP-based security.
8450.name: userful-splunk-plugin

8461.description: This application was created by Cisco CX to help customers visualize technical debt and manage known risk.  Version 1.1 introduced a single dashboard called Risk Overview which shows KPIs for Lifecycle risk, Vulnerability risk and Compliance risk.  Version 1.2 added Get Well Plan and Hardware End of Life Summary.  Version 1.3 added Hardware End of Life Details, Hardware End of Life Planning, and 4 Software Strategy dashboards.  Version 1.4 added Software Diversity Outliers, Software Diverse Families and Sparing Policy Alignment dashboards, bringing the total to 12.  Version 1.5 added SMARTnet Coverage, Crash Analysis and Hardware Inventory dashboards.  Version 1.5.1 added Unidentified Inventory Details, for a total of 16.
8461.name: lcs_insights

8467.description:
This app provides the index-time configuration to ingest the CDR data from Oracle Session Border Controller (Oracle SBC) and also from Oracle Session Router.     Specifically the CSV-formatted CDR format that you get if you enable local cdr storage on the oracle boxes.      The app correctly separates the intermingled start, interim and end records of this dataset,  into three distinct sourcetypes, and extract the fields from each correctly.

Note that although you can use it purely to onboard the data,   this app contains NO search-time config and will not by itself extract the search-time fields properly.    This is because it is designed to always work in conjunction with Sideview's "Supporting App for Oracle SBC", which is to be installed on the Search Head and which handles the search-time field extractions among other things.

Furthermore ,both of these apps together are also designed to work with Sideview's "Cisco CDR Reporting and Analytics" app.   When all three pieces are used together, the solution gives users a more general Investigation and Reporting solution for Oracle SBC and Oracle Session Router.

The use cases are actually nearly endless so please contact Sideview if you have questions or would just like to find out more.

this app is a part of Sideview's overall Investigation and Reporting solution, and you can find more documentation on the Sideview website:  https://sideviewapps.com/documentation/oracle-sbc-cdr
8467.displayName: Supporting Add-on for Oracle SBC CDR
8467.name: TA_oracle_sbc_cdr

8476.description: TA-brandefense is a Technology Add-on that pulls security data from the Brandefense Digital Risk Protection platform into Splunk. It collects incidents, indicators of compromise (IP addresses, domains, hashes, URLs), threat intelligence reports, and audit logs via the Brandefense API with incremental checkpoint-based collection and CIM-compliant field mappings.
8476.name: TA-brandefense

8477.description:
The App Compatibility Checker identifies installed apps that are not compatible with a selected target Splunk version and displays them on the Incompatible Apps dashboard.

It helps administrators:

 - Prepare for Splunk upgrades
 - Detect archived or unsupported apps
 - Reduce upgrade risk

The app only covers apps not built by Splunk. To check the compatibility status of Splunk-built apps use Splunk Health Assistant Add-On.
8477.name: app_version_check

8478.description: SA-brandefense is a Search Head app that provides dashboards, alerts, and Splunk Enterprise Security (ES)  threat intelligence integration for data collected by TA-brandefense. It includes 5 dashboards for incidents, IOCs, intelligence reports, and audit activity, plus saved searches that automatically populate ES threat intelligence lookups from indexed IOC data.
8478.name: SA-brandefense

8485.description:
The Spaces Add-on for Splunk enables the ingestion of location analytics, occupancy insights, device telemetry, and engagement data from Cisco Spaces into Splunk.

Organizations leveraging Cisco Spaces for indoor location intelligence, presence analytics, and asset visibility often need to operationalize that data within broader security, IT, facilities, or business analytics workflows. This add-on provides a secure and configurable integration that collects data from Cisco Spaces APIs and normalizes it for use within Splunk.

By bringing Cisco Spaces data into Splunk, organizations can:

Correlate physical space utilization with IT and security events
Monitor occupancy and device presence trends
Enhance operational visibility across facilities and campus environments
Support analytics, dashboards, and alerting use cases within Splunk

The add-on uses modular inputs to retrieve data from Cisco Spaces and leverages Splunk’s credential storage for secure authentication handling.

This add-on is designed for customers who want to extend the value of Cisco Spaces data into their Splunk environment for search, correlation, visualization, and operational intelligence use cases.
8485.name: ta_cisco_spaces

8486.description:
Managing STIG compliance shouldn't require expensive vendor platforms, complex infrastructure, or weeks of deployment. I built this app because I believe every organization — regardless of size or budget — deserves access to effective compliance tools. My goal is to help as many people and organizations as possible by providing a free, production-ready resource that solves a real problem.

STIG Compliance App for Splunk imports DISA STIG checklists and transforms them into searchable, reportable compliance data — with zero external dependencies and zero infrastructure beyond your existing Splunk deployment.

Upload .ckl and .cklb checklist files directly through your browser. The app parses, indexes, and visualizes your compliance posture in minutes — not days.

KEY FEATURES

- Browser-based drag-and-drop upload for .ckl and .cklb files — no forwarders, no file shares, no scripted inputs
- Custom CKL/CKLB parser converts DISA STIG XML checklists into searchable Splunk events
- Four compliance dashboards: Compliance Overview, Executive Summary, POA&M Report, and Upload History
- Risk scoring by severity: (CAT I x 10) + (CAT II x 5) + (CAT III x 1)
- Deduplication logic displays only the latest checklist per host/STIG combination
- Historical upload tracking with timestamps for audit trail
- Pre-built saved searches and alert templates for CAT I findings and compliance thresholds
- Works on a single search head or in a clustered environment — fully self-contained

WHY THIS APP EXISTS

Most STIG compliance workflows today involve Excel spreadsheets, manual tracking, or paid platforms that require additional licensing and infrastructure. This app eliminates those barriers. Install one app, upload through your browser, and get compliance dashboards immediately.
8486.name: stig_compliance_app

8494.description:
See if your company's data was leaked to the dark web with this app for the Dark Web Scan by Bechtle.

Credentials of your employees, customers, suppliers and service technicians get stolen by cybercriminals and are sold on the dark web. Get notified about any new offers or leaks regarding your company using this app.

- We permanently look out for new forums, marketplaces or chat groups and get access.
- Then we crawl data from these sources on the digital underground and save it to our database.
- You get notified if there's something about your company being sold.

Using the custom Splunk search commands, you can even customize your automations. For example, you could set up your own automation to send an email to users with leaked credentials or reset their passwords automatically. (The automation requires additional setup and is not part of the app.)

Additionally, the Dark Web Scan by Bechtle also allows you to search for common misconfigurations or other risks that could lead to security incidents in the future, e.g. email security settings, the security of your website against common cyberattacks or OSINT info like publicly discoverable email addresses or subdomains.
8494.displayName: Dark Web Scan by Bechtle App
8494.name: darkwebscan_app

8497.description:
TrackUC or Track use-cases is a splunk app to track and manage use-case lifecycle (creation, modification and approval) for security solutions. it provides a case management similar to splunk enterprise security ES.


the app also:
- supports search head clustering (SHC)
- Has friendly UI similar to splunk ES
- Protects approved tickets/requests from modifications
- Provides a dashboard to track SLAs and provide overview of created tickets/requests


* restarting the Search Head is required after installation
* only users with the role "trackuc_approval" can approve tickets/requests
8497.name: track-uc

8501.description:
CIS Benchmark compliance reporting shouldn't require a paid dashboard platform, a dedicated ingestion pipeline, or weeks of integration work.

I built this app because I believe compliance visibility shouldn't be gated behind expensive platforms or complex integrations. Whether you're a small team without budget for commercial tooling or a large organization looking for a lightweight alternative, you deserve a solution that works out of the box. My goal is to help as many teams as possible by providing a free, production-ready app that solves a real gap in the Splunk ecosystem.

Compliance Posture for Splunk is a free, self-contained Splunk application that gives you consolidated CIS Benchmark compliance visibility across your entire infrastructure — from install to dashboards in minutes.

Upload your CIS-CAT Pro Assessor ARF XML scan results directly through the browser using drag-and-drop. No file shares. No scripted inputs. No forwarder configuration. Just install the app, upload your scan files, and see where you stand.

Built by a Splunk Enterprise Architect with 10+ years of hands-on experience implementing compliance reporting solutions across commercial and federal environments.
8501.name: compliance_posture_app

8507.description: This Upstream Security Splunk native dashboard serves as the central "Security Posture" view, providing Security Operations Center (SOC) analysts with real-time visibility into the security status of connected vehicles, IoT devices, API endpoints, and consumers. It enables ingestion of cybersecurity alerts and telematics events from the Upstream Security platform into Splunk. It provides a modular input that polls the Upstream API and checkpoints progress to avoid duplicate events.
8507.displayName: Upstream Security App for Cybersecurity Alerts
8507.name: upstream-app-cybersecurity-alerts

8511.description: This app provides a mechanism to send custom webhooks from Splunk using a custom Python script, enabling full configuration of parameters that Splunk’s default webhook capability is limited in—especially custom HTTP headers (e.g., Authorization: Bearer ..., API keys). The goal of the app is to integrate Splunk with external systems (SIEM/SOAR, ticketing, incident management, chat ops, internal APIs) in scenarios that require authentication headers that Splunk’s built-in webhook cannot support.
8511.name: fpt_custom_alert_webhook

8514.description:
VIAVI Observer Apex App for Splunk: Turning Network Data into NetSecOps Actionable Insight.

Transform your network and security monitoring data into actionable insights with the Observer Apex App for Splunk. This powerful integration brings Viavi Observer Apex alerts directly into your Splunk environment, enabling real-time visibility into critical network or security issues.
8514.name: TA-viavi-apex-addon

8515.description:
Security visibility and analytics for Aviatrix Distributed Cloud Firewall in Splunk. Provides CIM-compliant field extractions and six pre-built dashboards for SIEM/SOC teams monitoring multi-cloud network security.

Dashboards included:
- Security Overview: Executive security posture with KPIs, threat timeline, top blocked destinations, and gateway block rates
- Traffic Analysis: L4/L7/FQDN traffic patterns, top sources/destinations, and protocol breakdown
- Threat Detection: IDS alert severity, Suricata signature analysis, and source/destination correlation
- Policy Enforcement: L7 policy hits, allow/deny ratios, and domain analysis
- Gateway Health: CPU, memory, disk, and network throughput monitoring per gateway
- Audit Trail: Controller API changes, user activity, and success/failure tracking

Supported log types:
- Aviatrix Cloud Firewall L4 micro-segmentation logs
- Aviatrix Cloud Firewall L7 TLS/SNI inspection logs
- Aviatrix Cloud Firewall IDS alerts (EVE JSON)
- Gateway network and system statistics
- Cloud Native Security Fabric API audit logs

CIM data models supported: Network Traffic, Intrusion Detection, Change Analysis

Requires the companion TA-aviatrix add-on for field extractions and CIM compliance. Logs are ingested via the Aviatrix SIEM Connector using Splunk HEC (HTTP Event Collector).
8515.name: aviatrix-security

8516.description:
Splunk Technology Add-on for sending **S/MIME signed and/or encrypted email** from:

- a custom SPL command (`smimemail`)
- a custom alert action (**Send S/MIME Email**) compatible with Splunk ES workflows

It includes UI pages for SMTP/OAuth2 setup and certificate management.

## Current Capabilities

| Capability | Status |
|---|---|
| S/MIME signing | PKCS#7 SignedData (detached), SHA-256 with RSA |
| S/MIME encryption | PKCS#7 EnvelopedData, AES-256-CBC content encryption |
| Recipient cert validation | Blocks send when recipient certs are missing/disabled (unless explicitly skipped in `smimemail`) |
| SMTP transport | `none`, `starttls`, `ssl` |
| OAuth2 mode | Uses client-credentials token and sends via **Microsoft Graph** raw MIME upload || Heavy Forwarder proxy | Route email delivery through an HF running **TA-smime-mailer-hf** || Alert action include options | Links, inline results, trigger metadata, CSV attachment |
| Certificate monitoring endpoint | `| rest /servicesNS/nobody/ta-smime-mailer/smime_cert_monitor` |
8516.displayName: Add-on for sending email utilizing S.MIME - signatures and encryption
8516.name: TA-smime-mailer

8517.description: Technology Add-on for Aviatrix Distributed Cloud Firewall logs. Provides field extractions, CIM-compliant data normalization, and lookup tables for Aviatrix security event data including L4/L7 firewall, FQDN egress filtering, Suricata IDS alerts, gateway telemetry, and controller audit logs. Maps to Network Traffic, Intrusion Detection, and Change Analysis CIM data models. Designed to work alongside the Aviatrix Security app for dashboards and visualizations.
8517.name: TA-aviatrix

8519.description:
About Us:

CyberCX is Australia’s greatest force of cyber security experts. Our highly skilled professional services team operates a 24x7 on-shore security operations centre (SOC) servicing corporate and public sector organisations across Australia and New Zealand, specialising in Security Operations services leveraging Splunk.

Description:
